context: Tests whether ask-matt correctly routes a concrete user situation — a customer-reported bug with a theory already formed — to a specific skill rather than listing all available skills. The correct primary routes are /triage-issue (to investigate and create a tracked, agent-ready issue) or /diagnose (if the user wants to deepen their debugging first). The SKILL.md flow map classifies both of these as standalone skills, not on the main idea-to-ship flow. The agent should name one skill with a clear reason, note that this is off the main flow, surface a natural follow-on (e.g. after /diagnose comes /triage-issue, or after /triage-issue comes /implement), and ask a clarifying question only if the choice genuinely hinges on information not provided.

checklist.name: Names a specific skill
checklist.description: The agent names exactly one primary skill to start with (e.g. /triage-issue or /diagnose) rather than listing all available options or presenting a menu. Award zero marks if the response is a list of skills without a clear recommendation.

checklist.name: Explains why that skill fits
checklist.description: The agent explains the fit between the user's situation and the recommended skill — not just what the skill does in the abstract, but why it matches this specific scenario (customer bug, theory already formed, wants a fix plan).

checklist.name: Correctly identifies this as standalone, not main flow
checklist.description: The agent correctly places the recommended skill off the main idea-to-ship flow (grill-with-docs → to-prd → to-issues → implement). It should not send the user to /grill-with-docs or /to-prd as the first step for a bug with a theory already formed.

checklist.name: Offers a natural follow-on skill
checklist.description: The agent mentions the natural next step after the recommended skill. For example: after /diagnose the user would move to /triage-issue; after /triage-issue the user would move to /implement. The follow-on should be named, not just implied.

checklist.name: Does not enumerate all skills
checklist.description: The response does not list or describe every skill in the repo. A clear single-path answer demonstrates that ask-matt is a router, not a catalogue. Deduct marks proportionally if the response names more than two skills total (primary + one follow-on).
